text: here are five more underrated but

again the subclass choices for the super

are the Assassin Rogue and Battle Master
fighter firstly action surge is amazing
when you get the drop on a surprised
enemy cuz it doubles the amount of
attacks you make that turn and all those
attacks are crits bug bears are an
awesome race for this by the way because
their extra damage is doubled too
fighter also gives the Rogue medium

defense fighting style if you want to
land range sneak attacks more often you
Ki yourself up with the archery fighting
sty style which combos amazingly with

with your repost maneuver and the
Sentinel feat you're totally set up to
double the rogue's damage output with
two sneak attacks per round repost lets
you immediately attack an enemy who
attacked you and missed sneak attack
triggers once per turn so if you can get
off a reaction attack via repost or an
opportunity attack on another creature's
turn you're rolling those clacky
endorphin rocks twice as
often oh that felt good you want to keep

don't go any higher than level six in
Fighter for extra attack and the bonus
feat assassin Battle Master is amazing
defense and damage wrapped up in one

Tempest or life cleric gets you heavy

the standard B plus clerics gets some

personal favorite is Tempest cleric lard
you grab two levels in Tempest cleric
you pick up your cantrips you grab your
heavy armor you get yourself blessed you

says whenever you would roll damage for

just get the maximum damage without
rolling that would be awesome except
Tempest clerics don't really get that
many good lightning and thunder based

grabbing lightning bolts now you have a
once per short rest 48 damage bolt of

potentially hitting multiple enemies at
higher levels you can steal chain

cutting words so the last thing they

description the Gloom fist monks are the
pickiest class to multiclass Wi they
hate giving up monk levels because it
reduces their key points but jamming a
bit of Ranger in there is actually worth
it firstly the two classes care about
the same stuff both of them live and die
by dexterity and wisdom so you give up
nothing in terms of of stats at level
one Ranger you get the canny ability for
expertise in any skill it's cute you
also get proficiency with all Marshall
weapons letting you wield a long sword
as a monk with your dedicated weapon
feature instantly pumping your damage
but the real fire is the spell casting
at level two and the ranger subass at
level three Hunter's Mark lets you deal
an extra 1d6 damage per attack and monks
attack more than anyone else you
probably want to start this build with
five LEL in monk speed running to that
extra attack then after two levels in
Ranger you're slamming down four attacks
per turn two of which are with a long
sword for 28 plus 66+ 20 damage with the
dualist fighting style that is hot and
it's not like monks were doing anything
with that concentration anyway a third
level in Ranger grabs the subclass that
ties everything together the Gloom
stalker on your first turn of every
combat you get an additional attack
which sort of offsets the downside of
using your bonus action to set up
Hunter's Mark and that extra attack
deals an additional 1d8 damage plus you
get a bonus to your initiative roles if
you want to power game the crap out of
this and I think that's kind of fair
you're playing a monk after all you can
be the bug bear race this gives you the
long limbed feature letting you attack

within the reach of your enemy attacks
it also lets you deal an additional 2 D6
per attack against any enemy who has
hasn't moved yet in combat that is a

Initiative for an insane first turn of

f wonderer is also a good subclass

can surround yourself with enemies
that's a lot of damage as for monk

and sets up all your allies for
Advantage as well because you'll be
going first thanks to your high
initiative the fiser the fiser is a

like the name suggests it brings some

clim firstly fighter plus artificer

cure wounds Shield plus 5 AC a d10 hit

a heavy crossbow you can multi-attack
with comboing amazingly with the
Sharpshooter feat and the archery
fighting style and enhanced defense is a
free plus1 AC and enhanced weapon is a
free plus one weapon basically whatever
your fighter is trying to do the fiser
does it better plus replicate magic item
is just amazing and then with your third

battle Smith or artillerist subclasses
depending on whether you want a pet
robot or a pet gun many fighters
struggle to find a use for their bonus
action but this multiclass solves that
by giving you permanent bonus action
utility every bonus action you're going
to be shooting enemies to death slamming
enemies to death or shooting allies to
life with a healing turret but maybe the
coolest thing about this entire
multiclass is the first ability of
artificers firearm proficiency if you
like the idea of guns and your DM is
cool with it a gun toting fighter is
badass with the repeating shot infusion
and it's a massive flavor win as well I
see the fiser as a gadgeteer type player
someone who enhances their combat with
Gadget
technology and brain power Batman with

somebody Divine favor is dedicated to
you it's there to stick around it
doesn't matter how many people you kill
you're going to keep that D4 bonus until

actions you can use to make attacks
equals more damage equals more dopamine

up and running after just one level in
